rationale: The two papers share a broad service-evaluation context (buyer–seller interactions and customer responses), so the 2010 article could plausibly be background for how contextual cues shape evaluations. However, the focal mechanisms differ (warmth/competence and conspicuousness vs. self-awareness and attribution), and the only match evidence is a single DOI reference with no citation-context text. Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop but not strong enough to clearly keep.
